Check: Coding of prior year refunds and prior year reimbursements
Suggested Method/Notes:
Excel file Obj Column: filter by 41980

Ensure that revenue object 41980 is truly for refunds issued to the LEA from vendors for returned merchandise or services pertaining to prior year expenditures and not for reimbursement requests for expenditures incurred during the prior year.  If the SBO claims they are for the latter, provide the below guidance.

Guidelines for revenue received in the current year that pertains to reimbursement requests for expenditures incurred during the prior year:
24xxx (PED Federal Flowthrough) – 44504 Federal Flow-Through Prior Year
25xxx (Federal Direct) – 44301 Other Restricted Grants - Federal Direct
27xxx (PED State Flowthrough) – 43204 State Flow-Through Prior Year
28xxx (State Direct)- 43203 State Direct Grants
other funds (discuss with supervisor what is appropriate)
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval and a reopen/reupload will not fix this as it requires a BAR(s) to fix.

Check: Current Period equals Current Period of SEG file from the FTS.
Suggested Method/Notes:
Excel file Obj Column: Filter on 43101

Excel file Current Period amount should equal FTS SEG File amounts in:

Quarterly Reporting -  Column "20XX-20XX QX TOTAL SEG PAYMENTS", except Local Charters Column "20XX-20XX QX TOTAL 98% SEG Local Charter PAYMENTS"

Monthly Reporting - Column "TOTAL SEG MONTH 20XX", except Local Charters Column "TOTAL SEG MONTH 20XX" multiplied by 98%

There are times when a mismatch could be accurate for example, in Q4 an budget entity who has not received June SEG in the month of June. This will also cause a mismatch in the following year Q1 as the entity will likely receive June SEG in July.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval if the explanation makes sense; otherwise, disapproval and resubmission  is required.

There are times when a mismatch could be accurate for example, in Q4 an budget entity who has not received June SEG in the month of June. This will also cause a mismatch in the following year Q1 as the entity will likely receive June SEG in July.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval if the explanation makes sense; otherwise, disapproval and resubmission  is required.

Check: Operational Fund - Construction and Building Acquisition Expenditures and Budget require Approval by Director
Suggested Method/Notes:
Fund Column: Filter on 11000
Func Column: Filter on 4000
Obj Column: Filter on 54500, 57111, 57112, 57200
Ensure that no amounts are listed in the YTD column or the Adjusted Budget column.

Make note and have discussion with SBO on what these expenditures entail.  Per statute 22-8-41 A school district shall not expend money from its operational fund for the acquisition of a building site or for the construction of a new structure, unless the school district has bonded itself to practical capacity or the secretary determines and certifies to the legislative finance committee that the expending of money from the operational fund for this purpose is necessary for an adequate public educational program and will not unduly hamper the school district's current operations.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: Review the cash report to determine if any values are entered on this line for any funds and if those values are appropriate.
Suggested Method/Notes:
Go the budget entity's subsite for the current year-->Permanent Cash Transfers folder and determine if we have an approved PCT or supporting documentation for a reversion.  The amounts listed on that or those PCTs should correspond, in total, to the values listed on this line.

If a PCT is in process or not yet submitted, line 6 should not record any values yet. It is worth noting that although not under SBB control, Transportation (Fund 13000) allows for a 50% reversion of their cash balance that does not require a PCT and most likely will be listed on this line.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval.

Check: Review for negative cash balances on non-reimbursable funds
Suggested Method/Notes:
Discuss with supervisor if non reimbursable funds are negative. Make note to SBO

Funds 27xxx (except 27109), 24xxx, should be negative.
Some funds within 25xxx, 26xxx, 28xxx, 29xxx, can be negative as they may be on reimbursement basis.
21000 and 21100 are on reimbursement basis based on a set rate and the number of meals served.  A budget entity can carry a cash balance and more than likely these funds will not be negative but they have the potential to be negative.
31200 is more often negative but recently there are awards not on RfR basis
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval.

Check: Payroll Liabilities should be positive and professional judement  if it is not a plug.
Suggested Method/Notes: This is a self reported cell; however, professional judgement should be used whether or not to question if these are true payroll liabilities or a plug to make the cash report balance.  These amounts should generally be positive.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval.

Check:
Line 11
Total Outstanding Loans
Suggested Method/Notes:
Ensure that the following conditions are met for this line:
1.Line 11 should always be >=$0 except in Fund 11000, which should be <=$0. Fund 11000 is the only fund that can temporarily loan other funds as the budget entity waits on reimbursement. Reimbursable funds have a negative line 10 and Operational loans the corresponding positive value to in order to cause a zero value in line 12.
2. The grand total of line 11 must be $0.
3. Fund 11000 should not loan to Funds 22000 and 23000 as these are self-sufficient funds, not reimbursement based and should not be negative. Conversation with SBO to move expenditures to Fund 11000.
4.  If Line 10 in fund 11000 is negative, the entity cannot loan to other funds as there is no available cash to loan the entity must reflect the actual fund in which temporary loans are being made. If line 12 in fund 11000 is negative, the entity must reflect the actual fund in which temporary loans are being made.  Discuss with supervisor.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: Bonds
Suggested Method/Notes: You can find active bonds here FTS: budget entity-->Bonds->Active Bonds.  If revenue is being receipted in either of these funds, ensure an active Bond is on file (the retirement schedule issued in current FY total amount should equal the amount of revenue receipted).  If this is not the case and revenue is being receipted here, there is probably an error with how the entity is coding bonds through NMFA.  The only time revenue should be receipted is for interest, investments, object 41980 (if it is a true refund) or sale of bond in the current fiscal year (not reimbursement from NMFA).  If a charter has an amount here, discuss with supervisor next steps.
Disapprove?: N/A

Check: Bank Statements are correct and ending balances matches the cash report
Suggested Method/Notes:
Review each bank statement in comparison to the values listed in the From Bank Statements section on the last page of the cash report.

The Account Name, Bank and Statement Balance are the only cells that can be verified.

Verify entity name, end date and ending balance on each statement. Ensure that the end date coincides with the end date of the period being reviewed, the budget entity is the budget entity being reviewed and the ending balance matches what is entered on the cash report for that account.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: Adjusted Bank Statement
Suggested Method/Notes: Ensure description and amount make sense; otherwise, have discussion with SBO on what the amount is
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: No Bank-to-Book Variance
Suggested Method/Notes:
Ensure that the entity is RECONCILED in column K after the bank Statements as noted by the 0.00 value.  Any other value implies a bank-to-book variance that the budget entity will have to research further and fix before approval.
The Total Outstanding Loans has another built-in check where the total amount must equal 0.00 or the cash report will state ERROR, denoting that a fix is required on these loans to offset one another.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: Reasonable Explanation Provided for Temporary Loans
Suggested Method/Notes: The first cells in that section should show the Fund 11000 with a negative amount and explanations as to what funds have been temporarily loaned cash.  The remaining entries should show the positive amounts that in sum total equal the amount of the negative amount from Fund 11000, each with an explanation.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: Reasonable Explanation Provided for Adjustments
Suggested Method/Notes:
Verify that language appropriate to the line is entered into the tan cells under the Adjustments section

This is not used to adjust to cash or to balance when the inability to reconcile exists.

Verify that values are only entered on this line for one of two things:
1. revenue recorded, but not yet received
2. an expenditure that has not yet been liquidated
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval.

Check: Cash Report on Website
Suggested Method/Notes: Ensure budget entity has the cash report on their website required per statute.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval.

Check: Audit findings
Suggested Method/Notes:
Until such time that audit becomes available, address audit findings as noted in Director or Deputy Secretary comments of the most recent operating budget checklist found in the FTS\SBB_Opbud_Review\FY##\3 COMPLETED Budget Review. Make note and have discussion with SBO on what the progress is on clearing the finding.

Once audit is available, review relevant SBB audit findings. Make note and have discussion with SBO on what the progress is on clearing the finding. If you are unsure if an audit finding is relevant, please discuss with your supervisor. Bring egregious audit findings to the attention of your supervisor i.e. material weakness findings, repeat findings not addressed for several years, modified/disclaimer opinions.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval.

Check: RfR Summary Report
Suggested Method/Notes:
OBMS: Reports --> RfR Reports --> RfR Summary Report
Select "Budget Year," for the period being reviewed; select "Entity" Name" click "View Report;" export to Excel by clicking on disk icon and clicking Excel.

Entity's should submit 2 RfRs per month per fund. Review the fund and submit date to see if the entity is being consistent with submitting RfRs timely.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval.

Check: Contact Info
Suggested Method/Notes: Each quarter, list the Business Manager and Superintendent/Charter Rep in the comments and ask SBO if this is still correct. If incorrect, please ask entity to correct the "Contact Info" file and place a copy in the Final reports folder, for example FY25 belongs in this folder Z:\SBB_Final Reports\Shared Documents\FY24\FY25 Contact Info
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: Other
Suggested Method/Notes: Any additional issues/red flags not captured in checklist that you find notable as well as the governance of entity, business manager responsiveness, etc.
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Suggested Method/Notes: Suggested Method/Notes TBD
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Suggested Method/Notes: Suggested Method/Notes TBD
Disapprove?: Not cause for disapproval unless entity asks to reopen/reupload

Check: OBMS Users Cert (Q4 Only)

Suggested Method/Notes:
The "Quarterly Feedback" sheet of this excel workbooks serves as the feedback memo.  On this sheet, please make rows larger so that all comments are visible. Your comments in this sheet in the "Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4 Findings Memo Notes" columns automatically transfer to the "Quarterly Feedback" sheet.  Ensure your comments contain distinct discussions on errors/issues and inlcude proposed corrective actions and/or reason(s) for withholding OBMS approval such as additional documents or fixes are required.  The notes should also address if a disapproval is required and why the disapproval had to occur.  Ensure you notify SBO of the completed feedback memo and request they provide their comments. There are columns for the SBO to provide comments on the "Quarterly Feedback" sheet.  

Save a copy of this excel using approved naming convention and in appropriate FTS folder for M/Q being reviewed.
Disapprove?: N/A

UCOA LINE/AREA of Report: Approve or Disapprove Actuals in OBMS
Suggested Method/Notes: In OBMS, navigate to the Actuals module, select Budget year, select Entity, select Reporting Period and then clicking Retrieve.  The Approve or Disapprove buttons will be directly below that.
Disapprove?: N/A
